tips.summary: Repeat a motion with a count 5j
details: Operators take counts too — 3dw deletes 3 words
Counts multiply: 2d3w deletes 6 words
{count} is the line number
Example: 120G jumps to line 120

tips.summary: Move by words w / b / e
details: w next word start
b previous word start
e moves to end of word

tips.summary: Jump to end of previous word ge
details: ge goes to end of previous word
Nice for edits before punctuation

tips.summary: Move by big WORDs W / B / E
details: WORDs split on whitespace
Great for paths and long tokens

tips.summary: Jump to end of previous WORD gE
details: gE goes to end of previous WORD
Useful on URLs / file paths
0 goes to the very first column
^ stops at first non-blank char
Lands on the last char of the line
Works as a motion: d$ deletes to end

tips.summary: Jump to last non-blank on line g_
details: g_ jumps to last non-space character
Handy near );}] and trailing spaces

tips.summary: Jump to text on next line + / -
details: + down to next line's first text
- up to previous line's first text
d+ deletes both lines
'[ first line of the last change or yank
'] jumps to its last line
`[ exact start of the last change or yank
`] jumps to its exact end
80| jumps to column 80
Great for line-length edits

tips.summary: Jump to file top/bottom gg / G
details: gg goes to first line
G goes to last line
Use a count before % to jump by file percentage
50% jumps to middle of file
10% jumps near the top
Bare % jumps to a matching pair instead
H top of screen, M middle, L bottom
Great for fast scanning

tips.summary: Move by screen lines on wraps gj / gk
details: gj and gk follow wrapped screen lines
Use them when one long line wraps

tips.summary: Wrapped line start or end g0 / g$
details: g0 jumps to start, g$ to end

tips.summary: First text on a wrapped line g^
details: g^ jumps past indent to the first text
{ goes to previous paragraph
} goes to next paragraph
Moves across blank-line-separated blocks
( moves to the previous sentence start
) moves to the next sentence start
Useful in prose, comments, and doc blocks
% jumps between (), {}, and [] by default
Use a count like 50% to jump by file percentage instead
Use to inspect a whole block quickly
[{ jumps to the previous unmatched {
]} jumps to the next unmatched }
Useful when you are inside nested code blocks
[( jumps to previous unmatched (
]) jumps to the next unmatched )
Useful inside nested expressions
`` returns to the exact previous spot
'' returns to the previous line (first non-blank)
Useful after big jumps like G, %, /, or file switches
Ctrl-o goes back through older jump locations
Ctrl-i goes forward again
Useful after searches, definitions, and other big jumps
`. jumps to the exact last change
'. jumps to the line of the last change
Useful when you want to get back to where you were editing
g; goes to an older change position
g, moves back toward newer ones
f; jumps to next ';' on the line
Great for long parameter lists
F; jumps to previous ';' on the line
Use to move precisely without searching

tips.summary: Move till before a character t{c}
details: t) stops just before ')'
Perfect for ct) edits

tips.summary: Move backward, stop after char T{c}
details: T) stops just after ')'
Good for edits near opening brackets
; repeats in same direction
, repeats in opposite direction

tips.summary: Drop a mark before exploring with ma
details: ma sets a mark before you jump elsewhere
`a returns to the exact position later
`a jumps to the exact marked position
'a jumps to the marked line

tips.summary: Go to definition with gd / gD
details: gd jumps to definition through the IDE
gD jumps to declaration when the IDE provides one
]m jumps to the next method start
[m jumps to the previous method start
Use ]M and [M for method ends
[[ jumps to previous section start
]] jumps to the next section start
[] and ][ jump to section ends
]s jumps to next misspelled word
[s jumps to the previous one
Requires active spell checking

tips.summary: Place line top/center/bottom zt zz zb
details: zt top, zz center, zb bottom
Use after searches and big jumps
Ctrl-d scrolls down half a page
Ctrl-u scrolls up half a page
Ctrl-f scrolls forward one page
Ctrl-b scrolls back one page
Ctrl-e scrolls down one line
Ctrl-y scrolls up one line

tips.summary: Scroll sideways by columns zh / zl
details: zh scrolls left, zl scrolls right
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Scroll sideways half screen zH / zL
details: zH and zL scroll half a screen
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Place cursor screen edge zs / ze
details: zs shows cursor at screen start
ze shows cursor at screen end
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off
zfip folds the current paragraph or block
Takes any motion, like zf} or zfa{
Works where IDE folding is available
Select text in Visual mode, then press zf
Easier than describing it with a motion
za opens the fold, or closes it if open
Works where IDE folding is available

tips.summary: Force a fold open or closed
details: zo always opens, zc always closes
Use when toggle isn't enough
zA toggles the fold and all inside it
One key for the whole fold tree

tips.summary: Force nested folds open or closed
details: zO opens every level, zC closes them all
Use when toggle isn't enough
zj moves to the next fold start
zk moves to the previous fold start
zd deletes one fold at the cursor
zD deletes nested folds there too

tips.summary: Fold all / unfold all zM / zR
details: zM closes all folds in view
zR opens all folds in view

tips.summary: Control fold depth with zr / zm
details: zr opens one fold level
zm closes one fold level

tips.summary: Resume insert at last edit gi
details: Lands you in Insert mode, not Normal
Picks up exactly where you stopped typing
